settings.heading: FAQ
settings.description: Answers to common questions about the Colic Assurance Program.

settings.question: How is the Arenus Colic Assurance Program different from other colic programs?
settings.answer: Arenus offers comprehensive coverage including medical and surgical colic, up to $15,000 lifetime coverage, horses with colic history, post-surgery horses, continued coverage after episodes, international travel coverage, and horses of all ages post-weaning.

settings.question: How can Arenus offer this level of coverage?
settings.answer: Our products work. Our digestive health formulas are all scientifically developed, veterinarian approved and clinically tested.

settings.question: What products are eligible for the Colic Assurance Program?
settings.answer: Assure Guard, Assure Plus, and Assure Guard Gold qualify. Enrolled horses receive recommendations for either Colic History Wellness Program or Colic Free Wellness Program.

settings.question: Why does my horse need both Assure Guard and Assure Plus?
settings.answer: The combination provides flexible dosing to manage recurrent colic, allows tailored support based on need, and reduces daily maintenance costs.

settings.question: Is there a purchase requirement for enrolling in CAP?
settings.answer: Yes, purchase required 48 hours prior to or within 5 days following enrollment notification acceptance.

settings.question: What age of horse can be covered?
settings.answer: Any horse once weaned and at least 5 months old is eligible, with no upper age limits though coverage limitations may apply.

settings.question: How long does it take for my enrollment form to be approved?
settings.answer: Approval occurs within 5 business days of completed form submission.

settings.question: Is there a limit to the number of horses I can enroll in the CAP?
settings.answer: No limit. Submit a completed form for each horse.

settings.question: Is there any monthly or annual fee to enroll in the CAP?
settings.answer: No fees. Enrollment is free with product purchase.

settings.question: Can horses residing outside the USA enroll in the CAP?
settings.answer: Horse must reside in USA or Canada to enroll. Both countries' horses are covered while in either nation.

settings.question: Can leased horses be enrolled in the CAP?
settings.answer: Yes, though the lessee/agent completes forms. Owner information is required and the owner is ultimately responsible.

settings.question: Can any veterinarian administer treatment for colic episodes?
settings.answer: Yes, any licensed veterinarian can treat. However, veterinarian-attended episodes are required for claim reimbursement.

settings.question: Do I need to report all colic episodes?
settings.answer: Yes. All episodes of colic while enrolled must be reported to the CAP Program, regardless of whether a claim is filed or not.

settings.question: What are the Annual Wellness Requirements for enrolled horses?
settings.answer: One physical exam by veterinarian per year, one dental exam with recommended treatments per year, annual veterinarian-administered vaccinations, and veterinarian-directed deworming program including at least two deworming administrations and one fecal egg count test yearly.

settings.question: Does Arenus pay my veterinary bills directly?
settings.answer: No. Owners must pay veterinarian. CAP provides reimbursement after proof of payment.

settings.question: Does the CAP offer reimbursement for medically treated (non-surgical) colic episodes?
settings.answer: Yes. Unlike other colic programs, medical and surgical colic episodes are eligible for reimbursement when your horse is enrolled in the CAP.

settings.question: What is the process for filing a claim?
settings.answer: Complete Claim Initiation Form within 14 days of episode, then Colic Reimbursement Claim Form. Primary care veterinarian completes their form; attending veterinarian(s) complete their forms. All forms and materials due within 30 days of treatment completion for reimbursement eligibility.
